Secret Sorceries
of Mind-Control
Copy
Part 2: 	Five Essential Copy
Elements
With Dan Kennedy
www.gkic.com
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 2 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	3
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Five Essential Copy Elements
I'm going to show you the five best types of copywriting elements for our
industry 	(information marketing).
Essential 	Copy Element #1: Envy and Greener Pastures
Number one 	is envy and greener pastures.
There is an old Earl Nightingale 	“greener pastures” 	story which, in case you
haven't heard it, I will tell it in its shortest, least interesting form possible just so
you get it.
The story is about the farmer who, 	every morning, 	his practice 	is 	to get his cup
of coffee and walk out to the highest hill on his farm and stand on it and look
around. Every morning he sees far in the distance his neighbor's farm and its
pastures are far greener than are his.
At his neighbor's farm his neighbor has the same
morning habit. He gets up in the morning, gets his cup
of coffee, walks all the way out to the highest hill in his
farm, stands on top of it and surveys the land around
him.
He is frustrated and disappointed every single day by
noticing that, 	in the 	distance; 	his neighbor's farmland is so much greener than is
his.
One day they meet each other in town at the feed mill and go across the street to
the bar to have a couple of drinks together and there they make a deal and so
they swapped titles.
Farmer A goes home to Farmer B's farm and Farmer B goes home to Farmer A's
farm. The 	next morning they each get up, get their cup of coffee and walk out to
1. ENVY
Greener Pastures
Insider Opportunity
Privilege
Page 1 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 3 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	4
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
the highest hill on their farms and are shocked and disappointed to notice that
their neighbor's farm land is so much greener than is theirs.
This is how everybody's mind works.
When I used to run mixed-dog mastermind 	groups 	– 	meaning one person's an
optometrist, the other person owns a shoe 	store; 	everybody in the group is in a
different business 	– 	one 	of the biggest things you had to do as a cop in that kind
of group was keep everybody from wanting to swap businesses.
Everybody's thing looks and sounds better at a distance than
their own, 	which is why it is easier to sell a 	“greener pastures”
story than it is an 	“improve what you've got” 	story.
In 	the GKIC product “Opportunity 	Concepts” 	we talk about the fact that almost
everybody in our industry tends to sell one of three
things:
 	Rescue
 	Repair
 	Improvement
That's really what they tend to sell. If you look at almost all copy in our
industry, you will find that it is either rescue, repair or improvement.
But that's all focused on 	“the thing I'm already in,” 	that's all focused on the thing
I've already got.
It's not focused on an entirely different opportunity, an entirely different
greener pasture; and it is always easier to sell greener pastures.
That doesn't mean it can't be about rescuing, repairing or improving the thing
they've already got; 	it just can't be presented as that.
You will often see in my swipe files, because I use it a lot, some sort of 	“replace
your business” 	story.
It is always easier to
sell greener pastures
Page 2 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 4 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	5
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Chiropractic practice, dental practice, restaurant, doesn't make any difference;
here's what we're going to do, we're going to replace your business with a
better business.
It's the same business, but we use replacement language in order to 	present it to
people.
If 	you remember the story and you think about your people as the farmers,
everybody is always more attracted to that which they see in a distance as 	more
desirable than what it really is.
So people look at other people's lives and think, “Oh boy, 	I wish I was that” 	and
often, if in fact they do get that, they discover that it's different but it isn't
necessarily any better than where they were.
They trade spouses and discover, 	“Huh, different maybe, but not so great.”
They switch 	businesses, they discover, 	“Well, look at this, this thing 	has 	got its
own little set of uglies and evils.”
But 	it is very easy to trigger that in people; much 	easier than to trigger the
desire to improve or the desire to repair.
The desire to replace 	with something that at a distance looks
much better is a much more powerful thing.
Linked to this is the idea of insider knowledge, insider opportunity, perk and
privilege of being able to get something, do something that a lot of other people
can't.
I'll show you an old one first; so this is 1921.
This is an ad for 	International 	Correspondence 	Schools 	– 	ICS 	– 	you 	see them
advertising today. The 	correspondence school business is alive and well; ICS 	is
alive and well.
Page 3 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 5 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	6
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
They were a client of mine in the 1980s, 60 years
after this ad. They'll be around, perhaps digital
eventually, but they'll be around forever.
So, this is a picture of people, husband with wife
and child at new home:
The 	Proudest 	Moment of Our Lives Had
Come!
The way to get that is with a new career, 	which
you pick from this list of new careers.
This story is much more believable attached to
new careers than if we attached it to something
that helped the guy with the hat do better in the career he was already in 	–
because that's harder for him to believe.
This is a more contemporary; 	this is now about place.
You look out your window past your gardener who is busily pruning the
lemon, cherry and fig trees amidst the splendor of gardenias, hibiscus and
hollyhocks. The 	sky is clear blue; the sea is a
deeper blue sparkling with sunlight; a gentle
breeze comes drifting in from the ocean,
cleaning and 	refreshing; as your 	maid brings
you breakfast in bed.
For a moment you think you have died and
gone to heaven but this paradise is real and
affordable. In 	fact, 	it costs only half as much to
live in this dream lifestyle as it would to stay in
your own home.
Full ad is available as Example 	#17 	in the
Exhibits
Full Version 	of the first page is 	available as
Example #18 in the Exhibits.
Page 4 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 6 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	7
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
This is a 16-page sales letter for the International Living Newsletter, 	which tells
you how to move to some godforsaken, bug-infested dangerous place in which
you need armed guards around the perimeter.
However the good news is you can buy the whole 	thing for 	$5.85 	a month.
When I lived in Phoenix, I was 	a 	road warrior; 	I traveled a lot and I flew
commercial. Often, you're flying home to Phoenix and the person next to you,
the question usually asked 	– 	just 	about the time that you can see, if at night, all
the 	lights, or 	by 	day you can see all the desert. The question asked is:
“Are you coming here on business or vacation or do you live here?”
“I live here.”
“Oh, you live here. Oh I wish I lived here.”
Then 	they tell you all the horrible crap about Wichita.
“I live in Wichita and oooh, four months out of the year you just freeze to
death.”
Four 	months out of the year in Phoenix you cook, but that's okay.
“Oooh, there's always snow and the people are all fat and ugly.”
So they do ten minutes about how horrible it is to live in Wichita and how they
wished they lived in Phoenix. Sixty percent of the people who move in Phoenix
leave after the first July. There's a big churn rate, 	by the way.
Of course I would always say to them:
“Well Wichita has these big, green signs with arrows on them to tell you
how to get the hell out of Wichita. You can leave any time you want.
The Federal Government has not yet done population apportionment and
rationing and all that to make you stay in Wichita. They'll get around to it,
but right now you can leave.”
Page 5 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 7 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	8
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
People don't get that. They have in their head that this place is paradise. 	Not
so, and certainly Costa Rica ain't. 	But the guy in Wichita; 	this sounds really,
really, really good to him.
The elitist thing, this is an old sales letter of mine
(example #19), you 	may 	have seen it.
Welcome to a ‘radical underground’
Who 	else wants to 	defy all normal, common,
ordinary, customary boundaries and restrictions
on making money 	– 	on the speed, the ease and
the independence?
Well, hell who doesn't? Which is the point, it's exactly
the point. 	There is a mandate in all this to be different.
Here is a current control from a client of mine,
HealthSource, Chris Tomshack; 	they franchise
chiropractic clients. This is a current control postcard.
If you are finally at the point when you are
thinking…
Enough with the seminars, the coaching programs,
the consultants, the pre-packaged single condition
marketing programs 	(for neuropathy, fibromyalgia,
etc.) 	Enough, enough, enough already. There 	MUST
be a better way.
Huh, well there must be 	a better way.
Go 	immediately here, watch the video and download the
free report, or call and listen to a brief recorded message
and we'll send the stuff to you.
The First 2 pages of this letter are
available as Example #19 in the
Exhibits
Full postcard available as Example
#20 in the Exhibits
Page 6 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 8 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	9
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
There's a big "No" sign over a picture of a seminar room with "Trust 	Me 	I’m a
Guru” 	written on the white screen.
This will invite them to a seminar held by a guru who will sell them a franchise.
The irony aside, there's a mandate to be different.
What greener pastures is all about is positioning what you do as
different from everything else being sold to your target market.
This requires some creative thought, it requires some disregard of irony, it
regards some ability to make the switch when they finally see what it is that you
are presenting.
It's really not another seminar, it’s a 	Discovery Day event and it's not coaching;
it's coaching packaged as a franchise. So, there is reality to the claim, but there
is also poetic license to the claim.
So the first thing you want to get is the mandate to be different.
As we've gone along, 	I've also given you some words. Another great word, this
is a client of mine, Chris Cardell. This is a really good word:
Finally.
It suggests that everything that they have seen up
until now is one thing and 	finally 	there is a different
thing.
It's a very good word. This is …
Finally: 	All 	Of Your Marketing Is Done For
You
For you … words I 	also 	gave you 	before.
Announcing the complete digital marketing system, the instant marketing
system to plug into your business to bring you 	…
Page 7 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 9 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	10
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Understand the mental picture they get?
Plug, I can manage that; I plug in crap all the time.
The instant marketing system to plug 	into your business to bring you an
endless stream of new customers. All of your essential marketing done for
you in just 30 days.
Now that meets our mandate to be different.
If 	you insist on presenting 	what you sell to your market
as just better, you 	are at extreme disadvantage.
They have heard 	“better” 	a lot. There is a mandate to present it as different.
Essential 	Copy Element #2: Urgency
The second item 	is 	Urgency.
We leave large numbers of people and enormous sums of
money at the point of being almost persuaded.
In 	the chain of events that have to happen for there to be an
exchange of value for money, the last one is they have to hand
over the money.
If you get 	them almost to the point of doing that but then you allow time for
them to consider it, most of them will go away.
That's why studying the old stuff is so cool because the old stuff had built in an
invitation to procrastinate because almost every response from a distance had
built-in delay.
Somebody heard a radio 	commercial; 	they had to wait until they could get to a
land-line phone to call; 	so they had an opportunity to decide not to call, to forget
to call.
2. URGENCY
The One That Got Away
Fears
Scarcity
Exclusivity
Page 8 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 10 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	11
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
If somebody's going to order from the 	International Correspondence School 	ad,
they've got to fill out the coupon and clip out the coupon, they've got to go find
an envelope, they've got to put it in an envelope, they've got to put a stamp on it,
they've got to put it in the mail.
Built-in delay.
We don't have that in our world today. You have all sorts of ways to eliminate
the forced delay.
But 	you have huge numbers of people still who almost get to the phone, spend
time on a website and then bail out on the order form, etc. etc.
Largely, if they do, they are gone.
In effect, you've really got to start all over.
So urgency is very important.
There needs to be a sense of now or never.
So here's your urgency list; 	here are the things, the copy elements most used to
create urgency.
There is some version of the 	“One That Got Away” 	story and pretty much
everybody knows one of these in their lives.
There's usually a passed-down family story about grandpa who Ray 	Kroc
offered him all the franchise rights to McDonalds for 	$1.10 	in 	Tupelo and he
turned him down. Almost every family has some version of that story.
If you're in Disney in Orlando and you drive out the kind of back end into
Kissimmee, there's now a customer gate there and there's a three-story
McDonalds on the left side as you are driving out. It 	has drive-in ramps up to all
three stories. It’s 	huge, it's glass, 	it's this mega, mega, mega 	McDonalds on this
piece of land that used to be just an employee entrance and exit back there and
there was nothing going on in Kissimmee.
Page 9 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 11 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	12
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
That 	was an empty piece of land that nobody really placed an enormous amount
of value on, none of the whole place.
Rodney Tolleson and I had an option on that piece of land which cost us 50
bucks a month to maintain the option 	– 	and a 	month came when neither one of
us had 50 bucks.
Every time I drive past the three-story McDonalds on the piece of land at the
backside of Disney and Kissimmee, 	I hurt. Even thinking about it now.
I like a lot of you but I probably wouldn't be here if I had just had 50 bucks.
So, everybody has a sense of one that got away. You can stimulate it, you can
create the emotional pain attached to it 	– 	one of our seven 	secrets 	of mind-
control copy.
There are fears of course.
Urgency is created by a fear of being
left behind, left out, being uncool.
Why does everybody go stand in line to get the new smartphone? They don't
want to be uncool. They don't want to be left behind.
A lot of people think it's because they want to be first. No, no, they don't want to
be last. They don't want to be late to the party.
Fear of regret, easier 	to stimulate older customers than younger customers by
the way.
Boomer senior population, great trigger.
Scarcity, simply it's going to go away and there's not going to be any more.
In the Hammacher Schlemmer mail order Christmas catalog, they are selling a
$200,000 Batmobile 	– 	a 	brand new one. What 	a lot of people don't know is they
make some of those every single year. They make new ones and they sell them.
Page 10 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 12 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	13
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Most people would assume there's only one, so there's an urgency to get it
before you can't get it.
It's not stated; 	it's just, 	in this case, 	an assumption most people would make.
I happen to know they make a dozen every year, so if somebody got there first
they would still take your 	$200,000 	for the next one; but scarcity.
Exclusivity 	is 	like the next best thing to scarcity.
 	Scarcity 	is, it's all going to go away and there won't be any more period.
 	Exclusivity 	is, there's a limited amount of it and only a certain number of
people will be able to get it and then it will stop.
That, 	by the way, 	is a very good trigger with relatively 	affluent customers,
successful people. Because after all what is the point of being rich except to
have things other people can't have?
That's the whole point, so that trigger works pretty well.
Here's some actual copy. 	Here's a fear of regret template.
If you went through all of my work for 40 years, you would find this reused six
bazillion times in client copy.
It's not every day that a millionaire 	_____________ 	knocks on your door and
offers to 	______________.
You fill in the blank 	– 	a millionaire plumber, a 	millionaire real estate investor;
it's not every day that a 	– 	whatever, fill in the blank 	– 	arrives, knocks on your
door and offers to…. 	take you under their wing and reveal their secret formula
to you.
That's a template and it triggers fear of regret because it's not every day. So if I
pass this up what will happen? How will I feel twenty years from now if I did
not take advantage of that offer?
Page 11 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 13 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	14
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Here's 	“One 	That 	Got 	Away” 	copy. It's fairly recent, I wrote this for a client
just a few weeks ago.
An early partner of Jeff Bezos’ 	exited Amazon with a payment of $500. Tom
Monaghan's original partner in Dominos quit and took a used Volkswagen.
VISION ISN'T EASY. AS A RESULT, MANY PEOPLE LET THE OPPORTUNITY
OF A LIFETIME SLIP THROUGH THEIR FINGERS AND LIVE TO REGRET IT;
but in this case I think I could make it easy for you 	NOT 	to let this one slip
through your fingers…
Then 	it 	goes into risk-reversal guarantee copy of course, 	“so that you can take
advantage of this opportunity of a lifetime with zero risk.”
So that's 	One That Got Away 	copy. I'll show you another piece. This is very
current. Here's some urgency copy. This is toward the end of a long sales letter.
(you can see 	the last 2 pages in Example #21)
Let me end by telling you why I think your future hangs in the
balance, why this is so vitally important 	NOW.
We live in very uncertain times. Think back to the Lehman 	Brothers
weekend of death and the crash of the 	entire US economy that followed.
Maybe the crash in your 401K and IRA too …
Further down…
This is no time to be skating on such thin ice. This is no time to be playing
around with untested ideas and untested leaders. This is the time 	to
strengthen your practice.
It continues:
If you, 	in honest time with yourself, feel that there are weaknesses and
vulnerabilities in your practice; if you feel anxious about having enough
new patients; if you worry that your practice won't be valuable enough …
Page 12 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 14 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	15
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
They might not have been worrying about that stuff until I just said it, now
they're worrying about that stuff. And here's your urgency copy,
Please act decisively, the urgency is real, the deadline is real, discounts and
bonuses end with it. The likelihood the summit sells out early is also real.
The benefits of being here are 	proven. The timing is vital.
That's urgency copy that creates anxiety. 	Back to our 	seven 	secrets of mind-
control copy, it's urgency copy that creates anxiety, uncertainty, angst and,
when people are anxious, what do they want to do? Be un-anxious.
An act can occur that relieves the anxiety.
What's the act? Sign 	up, purchase. The purchase is the solution.
Now in reality, 	of course, 	if the guy doesn’t come to the seminar and take home
the big list of stuff to do 	– 	because the five elegantly simple things have fifty sub-
things 	– 	if 	he doesn't go home and use this, 	he isn’t 	really.
But he feels at that moment all that anxiety you created, he feels it all go away
because he has filled out the registration form and popped it into his fax
machine.
This is the game we are in.
Essential 	Copy Element #3: Contradiction
Third … here's a fascinating category of copy 	… it is called contradiction copy.
Contradiction copy says, which were 	you 	going to believe, your lying eyes or
me? Contradiction copy says, 	“No, the sky is red.”
For example, people want security and 	safety; 	they believe there's safety in
numbers 	– 	it's a foolish belief but people believe it.
Page 13 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 15 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	16
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
People who are of my age when parents exercised some control over your
dating 	– 	a 	foreign concept now I realize 	– 	mothers got their daughters to double
date because there was safety in numbers.
Actually it just provided people to take turns as a lookout; but they believed
there was safety in numbers.
However the problem with safety in numbers is,
it's not new and exciting, because if there are six
bazillion people doing it…
So we 	have to, 	with copy, 	be able to say that there
is safety in numbers but you are still 	getting in on
the ground floor and getting secrets nobody
knows about.
We have to be able to pull that off.
People want things that are tested and proven.
Nobody likes to be a lab rat or a guinea pig. However, people also want a new
breakthrough.
We have to be able to put those two things together in the same sentence and
make them work.
 	Secret, tens of thousands however are in on it.
 	Wall Street is evil, however when you invest in stocks with my advice,
you win.
 	Everybody there is a crook, it's a giant casino; 	all they do is steal your
money. Now come with me and let's go play in the casino.
Bad timing is good timing. Almost everybody by the way thinks the timing to do
whatever it is you want them to do right at that 	moment is bad.
3. CONTRADICTION
Safety in numbers /
In on the ground floor
New /
Tested and proven
Secret /
Tens of thousands are ….
Bad timing =
Good timing
Page 14 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 16 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	17
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
It's 	either generally bad 	– 	“The economy sucks, our industry's in trouble” 	– 	or
it's specifically bad 	– 	“I’ve 	got four young kids at home, I should wait until they
all …”
There's a great joke about the 90-year-old couple in front of the judge in divorce
court, “Why on earth have you waited? You've been married for 55 years.”
“We’ve 	been waiting for the kids to die.”
Here's some contradiction copy:
I have personally done more than 1,200 	transactions; 	I continue to actively
buy, sell, rehab, flip, find for others and invest right now as my business.
That's important. I'm not just getting a few deals ready to claim I'm doing
what I teach. 	This 	is my business.
NOT 	AS SEEN ON TV
I'm nothing like the 	“get 	rich 	in 	real estate” 	hotshots you see on late night
and weekend TV.
I have participated in nearly $200 	million of real estate transactions. 	I've
done a lot of small, nicely profitable deals. My biggest was acquiring 	$9.8
million of rental properties at half price.
I did nothing but deals from 2003 to 2008, 	when I started coaching other
investors. Then 	I built the most completely automated business system this
industry has ever seen, so you can do 	all your business on your smartphone.
Then I built the nation's largest, unique network of active investors and
buyers so that there's someone waiting for every good deal any student of
mine finds.
This is important. Only after perfecting my methods from 2003 to 2008 did
I then start sharing my knowledge with others.
Page 15 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 17 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	18
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Everything 	we've just said is old. It's tested, it's proven; 	it dates back to 2003.
Everything I've just said up until now has cobwebs on it.
Now, with all that as foundation, 	I've built a 	RADICALLY NEW SYSTEM 	that
produces profits 	in 	ANY 	market, good or bad, in 	ways no one in the real
estate industry has ever imagined possible.
This is so different from anything this field has ever seen. There are plenty
of skeptics and deniers, there are even haters 	– 	because they work so hard
and put up with so much suffering for their money 	while 	I and my students
are making money so easily. What they think, heck what they don't want
you to know, doesn't matter.
This kind of copywriting 	– 	if 	you really examine it again and again and again and
again 	– 	it's the same ingredients being put in the pot and put together every
single time.
What'd I wind up with? I wound up with secrets they don't want you to know.
Never before has this business been made so plug and play.
Plug, people get that. I can plug in something. I've managed to do that three or
four times without electrocuting myself.
You can simply find qualified properties that
can be bought in certain 	ways, post them to
our members-only online media, like using
match.com to attract dates or monster.com to
attract job interviews…
That's a copy technique 	not on 	my list 	but 	you want
to remember it, it's 	called 	“Tastes 	Like 	Chicken.”
You 	know the answer to everything, every horrible
thing anybody wants to get you to eat 	– 	rattlesnake, road-kill, fried warthog,
whatever 	– 	your question is, 	“Well what does it taste like?” 	and their answer is
You 	have to take
something that
people don't
understand and
hook it to 	something
that they do
understand
Page 16 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 18 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	19
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
always, 	“It 	tastes like chicken.” To 	which my answer is, 	“Well then, 	let's eat
chicken!”
However, the 	Taste Like Chicken 	copy technique is, you have to take something
that people don't understand and hook it to something that they do understand.
I didn't have it on 	my list; 	it's a minor matter, not one of the major five, but it's
still a good technique to know.
… 	like using match.com to attract dates or monster.com to attract job
interviews, have buyers come forward, make the match, make money, never
actually own the properties, let alone play landlord, borrow from banks, or
otherwise do difficult risky or time-consuming work.
That last paragraph is from our seven secrets of mind-control copy 	– 	“You 	can
get everything you want and do nothing you don't like.”
I'm releasing my new system to a very limited number…
I 	made 	it 	“old tested, proven, reliable as a Rambler” 	and then I made it “radically
new and secret and exclusive.”
Contradiction copy.
So here's a control piece. It's been a control piece now for five years for a client
of mine. It may well be one of the best pieces I've ever written 	– 	in 	terms of
results, certainly not in terms of creative genius because it is the kitchen sink
piece of all time.
It is taking every single thing we talk 	about 	in the 	seven secrets of mind-control
copy 	and every single thing we talk about 	here 	and shoving it into the same four
pages.
It is also all pieces of previously used copy from somewhere else stuck together
in the same piece. This is me with 3x5 cards and glue. This is what this is. So it
ain't elegant and it is full of contradiction copy about the economy.
Page 17 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 19 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	20
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
This is my 	“Let President Obama 	Make 	You 	Rich” 	copy.
The 	client has never let me do, 	“Let
President Obama 	Make You Rich and
You'll be the Only One”. He thought that
was counterproductive.
This is a PURLs 	piece 	– 	it 	drives 	you 	to a
website with your name in it.
Crazy Texas 	Millionaire
Crazy Texas 	Millionaire, 	I've used off and
on for these clients for 22 years.
I took it out of the stack, stuck it in here. 	Inside, 	we pick that back up:
My name is Phil Grove, I'm a nationally known businessman, author and
coach to experienced and brand-new business people, maybe you’ve heard
of me.
By the way, that's a great line. 	“Maybe you’ve heard of me.” 	They haven't but it
suggests everybody else has and they are just out of it.
My friends call me the Crazy Texas 	Millionaire but believe me I'm crazy like
a fox and I have turned the lousy economy into an amazing new
opportunity.
Crazy Texas 	Millionaire has been the 	whole 	ad in the past but here it is stuck in
this. Then I have:
Let President Obama 	Make You Rich
Free celebrity. Here's the contradiction copy:
This bad economy is sticking around and it may get worse but it 	could be
your best friend. Because of the lousy economy, 	there is a ton of money that
Page 18 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 20 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	21
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
could be going into your pocket right now easier than ever and I will help
make it happen.
By the way what do all people who aren't getting the financial results they want
in life believe?
“There's a bad economy.” 	Not 	“I'm screwed up.” 	No, no, no, it’s a bad economy.
So this is a given.
Crazy Texas 	Millionaire 	SPILLS THE BEANS 	on how 	insiders 	are profiting
big-time from the real estate crash and lousy economy.
Understand here, we are selling real estate investing and I have just used the
words 	“real estate crash.”
This is like inviting you to my restaurant with words like toxic gas and poison.
Contradiction copy.
Another 	piece I have, "Unusual money-making hobby." Hobbies 	is a 	good word
too because it sounds like not much work.
You don't want to say, 	“Unusual money-making work.” 	That's a bad idea. Hobby
has a nice connotation.
Essential 	Copy Element #4: Unconditional Love
Next, unconditional love 	copy. This is pretty essential. First of all it absolutely
cannot be their fault.
The most unwelcome message in the world is
“Your unsatisfactory condition is caused by you.”
This is an 	unpleasant, unappealing, pretty much unsalable proposition.
It's largely true, but it is an unsalable proposition.
Page 19 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 21 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	22
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
So 	not only don't you ever want to make it, you want to be as far at the opposite
end of that continuum as you possibly can be.
It 	can't be their fault and you want to tell them it's not their fault.
You can loop it back to 	the seven secrets of mind-control copy 	under mysticism
to conspiracy if you wish; 	that's one way to get there.
But 	you just cannot tell a poor person they're poor because they choose to be
poor. You cannot tell a fat person they're fat because they choose to be fat.
It's an unacceptable thing to do. It's been tested by the way a lot.
Halbert tried a campaign telling fat people that they're fat because they were fat.
He did it as well as any copywriter could possibly write it, but it bombed
because nobody wants to hear that.
They wish to hear this opposite. Their 	unsatisfactory situation… 	whatever that
is, their relationship, their health, their physical fitness, their finances, their
annoying customers, their annoying employees… 	whatever their unsatisfactory
condition is, 	you win a lot of points when you hug
them and tell them, “It's not your fault.”
That's what their mama did 	– 	which has caused a
lot of their problems.
She hugged them and said, “It's not your fault. 	All
the other children are evil and mean. It's not your
fault, people just don't understand you.”
The guy is moving back home for the fifth time
after his fifth divorce and mama is telling him, “It's not your fault, all those
women are evil.”
At one it's a 50/50 proposition, at two… 	at five the common denominator in this
equation of misery is not the five spouses; 	it's you Bubba.
4. UNCONDITIONAL LOVE
It’s not YOUR fault
Your past doesn’t matter
I understand you
I 	appreciate your struggle…
You deserve…
Page 20 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 22 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	23
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
It's mathematically impossible to pick five evil bitches in a row. Can't do it.
So when we hug them with our copy and we tell them it is 	not their fault for
whatever reason we 	now 	have:
 	There are secrets that have been withheld from them.
 	There is a conspiracy against them.
 	Every 	way they have been shown so far to do X is so impossibly difficult
and ridiculous that only a few people could 	possibly do it.
For whatever reason, when we hug them with our copy and tell them it is not
their fault, we gain a lot of points, like all the points we ever need.
If you try telling them the other thing, you lose all your points.
The 	copy hug is:
“It’s 	not your fault that you are in this unsatisfactory situation because_______”
Now 	fill in the 	“because” 	blank 	and that's your copy
template.
It 	needs to feel kind of like a little hug, a little pat and
a little reassurance to whatever degree you can say
it.
Next, they have to be told their past doesn't matter.
One 	of the great lines in Personal Power I 	– 	the first
Guthy-Renker infomercials with Tony 	Robbins 	– 	one
of the great lines written in that show is, "Your past need not control your
future."
What a great line. I can't 	take credit for it, I think it's 	Lenny Lieberman's but
nonetheless, it is a summation of what people must believe to have optimism
about the future. Because the reason they're paying attention to you in the first
place is their past is full of unsatisfactory outcomes.
The 	reason they're
paying attention to
you in the first place
is their past is full of
unsatisfactory
outcomes.
Page 21 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 23 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	24
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Here's who will not be at your free preview about 	How to Get 	Rich in 	Real
Estate: 	Trump.
He ain't coming; he's got a fairly satisfactory outcome in the money department.
The people who are there have unsatisfactory outcomes in their past and, 	the
older they are, the more of them have accumulated.
The knapsack just gets bigger and bigger and bigger and it has more weight the
older they get because they can see the end.
So we have to tell them, don't worry about any of that.
Don't worry about the garage full of soap and vitamins you have, don't worry
about any of that. Oh, ex-con, no problem. Your past doesn't matter.
Next, I understand you. They want to engage in dialog with somebody who gets
them, and not really gets them, because 	if you really get them, you 	know all their
unsatisfactory outcomes are all their fault, they're probably incompetent.
They're going to be a major project to help.
They don't want you to understand them at that level, they want you to
understand how they feel and the difficulty of 	their life's struggle. Because
everybody thinks they are engaged in an epic struggle.
Most 	people have no idea of course. First of all if you were born here or
someplace like here… 	we can make a list of countries that if, 	by accident of birth
you were born there, you would flat out know struggle.
Everybody thinks they're engaged in an epic struggle
and so they want to be understood.
One of the ways you know you've got this right is anecdotally when people say
to you… 	and in the key markets in which I write copy for I get it a lot… “It
sounds like you were under the kitchen table at 2 o'clock in the morning when I
and my wife were having our conversations.”
Page 22 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 24 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	25
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Now you have to know what that conversation sounds like in order to get that,
which is why it's harder to write copy to groups you have no affinity for than it
is to write copy for groups you do have affinity for.
If you are going to write copy by the way for people you have no affinity for, you
need to go hang out. So 	if you're going to write copy for engineers who have
corporate jobs and you aren't one of them, you need to go find the bar down the
street from the Motorola factory and you need to for the next two weeks hang
out, dress yourself up like an engineer 	– 	kind of dull look on your face, gray skin,
pocket protector, the most complicated smart phone known to man with six
antennas, shoes with the little holes in the toes and laces 	– 	and 	go in there and
hang out from about 4 o'clock on.
You’ve 	got to do it because you’ve got to hit this.
There’s an old… the 	first time I heard 	this 	I thought 	it was on 	the list of stupid
stuff you hear from people. It goes 	back to Cavett 	Robert. 	His 	line was, "They
don't care how much you know until they know how much you care."
I had that 	negative 	reaction to five or six things Cavett 	said and it turns out
they're all 	wise, and this is one of them.
If they don't feel like you get them and you really care about
them, then you have an uphill battle.
I appreciate your struggle.
If you read carefully, you will see these two pieces personally in every book and
many issues of our newsletter.
You will see me do it again and again and again and again. I do understand the
small business owner and the entrepreneur and I do appreciate your struggle
every day, I get it.
Page 23 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 25 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	26
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
I live with exactly the same conspiracy against productivity and
accomplishment that you do. Every morning we leave the house and people
start throwing barriers and hurdles and roadblocks 	and orange cones and
everything in front of us. I get it.
By the end of the day even though you didn't lift a heavy object, you are
whipped. I get that.
Entrepreneurs want to know I get it. So I keep telling you so you know I get it.
You deserve 	to be 	(whatever the satisfactory exceptional outcome 	is you're
promising.) 	You deserve to be rich, you deserve a break today, you deserve to
be loved, you deserve to be thin, you deserve…
People are hard-wired to get entitlement as love; 	that's why once 	we started the
entitlement society it is so hard to put that Pandora back in the box. Because
every time somebody steps forward politically and creates a new entitlement, 	it
is perceived as love and caring and affection.
It gives people a warm feeling that these people in Washington or in the 	State
capital care so much about them that they are going to create and give this to
them. You remember Bill Clinton's 	“I feel your pain.”
“You deserve” 	are another two very powerful words. Put them on your word
list.
The founders gave us what? 	“Opportunity and the right to pursuit of.” 	What
have people translated it to? 	“Supposed to have.”
A home is a right, not the opportunity to maybe get one. No, no, no, 	a 	home is a
right. Health care is a right.
The extension of course… 	well you have a car, I don't have a car; 	we’ve 	either got
to cut your car in half so we each have half a car or you've got to share your car
with me or you've got to go buy me a car because I deserve a car. Why do you
deserve a car 	and I don't deserve a car? 	I deserve a car.
Page 24 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 26 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	27
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Then the next extension is, you've got a better car than I've got; 	well that's no
fair. I deserve a nice new Lexus just like you've got a nice new Lexus. Why
should I drive an old Chevy? 	The fact that I don't have a job and I don't work is
irrelevant.
This 	is in there; 	it's easy to stimulate and you want to stimulate it, so 	“You
deserve.”
Let me show you a piece of copy that falls into this category as well as some
others.
This is also very current copy, very current.
It's for a PURLs 	postcard so it says 	“insert name” 	at the
top.
If your 	spouse is threatening to divorce you if you
go 	to one more seminar or buy one more course
…
By the way I just hit about 75% of the people we're
mailing. See I know the conversation, I know that
conversation; 	“No you are not going to another
seminar Charlie.”
If your spouse is threatening to divorce 	you if you go to one more seminar
or buy one more course, may I have a few words with you?
Enough 	Learning, How ‘Bout Some Doing?
One weekend real estate seminar just makes you dangerous to yourself and
others. 	But 	trying to learn everything before making money puts you on a
treadmill to nowhere forever getting ready, never getting going. Get the
truth about making money now.
Full page version available as Example
#22
Page 25 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 27 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	28
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Why do so many with honest ambition never get started, stall or fail at real
estate investing?
I'm about to make it not their fault. The hug is coming.
Why do so many with honest ambition never get started, stall or 	fail at real
estate investing?
So 	here are the real reasons: They don't follow the instructions. They go to the
seminar, they buy some crap from Ron or whoever and I say crap like I say glop.
I don't mean it like manure, I mean it like our stuff.
They 	buy some stuff and they take it home and they put it under the bed and
they sleep above it. Then they check their bank account in a month and see how
many real estate transactions they’ve done. That's why.
Or, they go out and the very first person who says no to them, that's it. They can
only take one no.
But we're not going to say that. There's no profit in saying that. We're going to
give them a hug.
Why do so many with honest ambition never get started, stall or fail at real
estate investing? 	It is after all the cornerstone of all wealth. You know
people succeed at flipping 	houses; 	there are 	even reality TV shows about it.
By the way that's the same as 	“maybe you've heard of me.” 	You know, well I
didn't know, but since you said I know, I guess I know.
You know people who've grown rich buying, holding and renting
properties. Yet I will tell you my own study of this shows fewer than 15% of
the people who try succeed.
With that in mind I decided to do something to let earnest people
apprentice and earn while they learn and be guaranteed that they actually
make money, not just spend it on 	seminars and courses.
Page 26 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 28 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	29
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Who am I and then there's some credential copy.
… and so if you are tired of learning, preparing, waiting, maybe
procrastinating and ready to really get ahead financially, I'm ready to take
you under my wing.
Here are the four reasons people don't get anywhere at this and how I have
fixed them.
All four reasons have nothing to do with personal initiative, responsibility,
discipline, application of 	information, they have nothing to do with any of that.
They have to do with flaws in the way everybody has been told to do it up until
now.
Essential 	Copy Element #5: Reasons-Why I’ve Arrived
Copy
The last category 	is reasons-why I've arrived copy.
If we told the truth, here would be our reason why: 	“To get your money.” With
value in exchange, 	but to get your money.
So if there's 15,000 people in an arena and I'm the last
speaker of the day and I walk out and the first thing I say is,
“Just so we're clear, the only reason I'm here this afternoon
is to get money from as many of you as humanly possible,”
exactly how many points do I get for such honesty?
Not zero. It would appeal to a certain number of people; they would be awe-
struck by the candor, but not enough of them to feed very many horses and I
work for oats and vet bills so I have to keep that in mind.
There are 26 children back home who must be fed. 	So, that's not an option.
I can't start a sales letter that way, I can't start a webcast that way; 	I can't do any
of that that way.
5. REASONS-WHY I’VE
ARRIVED
Logical
Mission
Mystical
Page 27 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 29 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	30
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
The 	big lie at the other end is, 	“I love this so much I would do it for free.”
Really? Offer them an opportunity to do it for free. See how many leap.
I referred to him earlier, our friend Wayne Dyer, who even can put himself in
four places simultaneously, wouldn't take that offer up from any of the four!
No, but that kind of candor; 	people want to believe that there is a different
reason for why you do what you do and for why you have arrived in front of
them at this moment in time. And 	hopefully by the way there is another reason.
Hopefully you are doing what you're doing and arriving because you genuinely
want to deliver benefit, you are interested in what it is that you do.
Hopefully there are 	other reasons. But the number one reason if we're business
people has to be a business reason.
And by the way if your number one reason is not a business reason, you would
be well advised to be involved in social work, charity work, religious work,
whatever; not in business because you're dangerous to yourself. You make all
sorts of decisions for bad reasons.
So, they want to know why you have arrived in front of them and there are three
categories of answers.
One is the 	logical 	category. 	“I'm here because you’ve been an 	Entrepreneur
magazine subscriber for five consecutive years and based on that I assume X, Y
and Z. That's a logical reason why I've arrived in your mailbox.”
So, if my research about you is right, you were not picked at random 	– 	which can
or cannot be followed by how we picked you.
We need smarter plumbers; 	we need real estate finders; 	we need chiropractors
to handle 1,000 new patients a month in Summitville, Pennsylvania, right now.
Page 28 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 30 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	31
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
The “Let President Obama Make You Rich” 	piece that I showed you also has that.
It 	is person specific and area specific so it is dropped in and … "just for finding
certain homes in the Simpsonville area you get paid…"
Somewhere in 	there it says, "We desperately need somebody in Simpsonville
right now."
Those are logical reasons.
A second category of reason is 	mission 	reason.
“I'm mad as hell so I've come out of retirement because all these charlatans are
selling you crap and 	it aggravates me beyond all belief.
Here I am now to stand against the charlatans and the fakers and the pretenders
and give you the true secrets about commodity investing.
Every time I open 	Investor’s 	Business Daily and see one of those crackpot, lying
ads, it sets my hackles afire. So 	even though I have no need to work, I'd rather
not work, my wife doesn't want me to work, I'm here because I can't stand to
see good, honest people ripped off by these liars.”
That's the, 	“I'm mad as hell” 	copy.
I'm out 	to prove. 	“I’m sick and tired of all this
malaise and nobody can be self-reliant anymore.
Opportunity in America is dead and the American
dream no longer exists. 	I lie awake at night with the
frustration of all this so I'm out to prove all the
doubters wrong and take 1,000 ordinary men and women and make them
multi-millionaires in 36 months or less. Do 	you want to be one of the …”
That's the 	“out to prove.”
There's the 	“giving something 	back”. 	“I'm here because baseball has been very,
very, very, very good to me.” 	I'm here to give something back.
They 	want to know
why you have
arrived in front of
them
Page 29 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 31 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	32
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
There's a headline, 	“I 	Want to Give This to You Before It's Too Late.” 	It was 	the
headline of a full page ad in the 	Opportunity 	World.
The ad ran for 30-some odd years every single month in six or seven magazines.
It was a great story about a guy who now had been told he only had six months
to live. The ad ran for 33 years!
Well it said he had been 	told 	he only had six months to live. He 	didn't want to
take his secret to the grave and so 	“I want to give 	this to you before it's too late.”
These are mission reasons.
Then we have the 	mystical 	reasons. 	The right audience really likes the
mystical reasons.
That's some version of, 	“When the student is ready the teacher will appear,”
which is actually when 	the student is ready the salesman will appear.
Somebody has brought us together in this place, 	in this moment in time, 	for a
mystical reason. Pick the 	“somebody” that 	works for you and/or more
importantly for your audience.
It could be God, it could 	be the universe, it could be the magic frog Frodo; 	I don't
care, but that's that story.
This 	is a great line of copy, it's in a current sales letter of mine:
Trust 	What Your Inner Mind Tells You
Which is what we just told you your inner mind is saying to you by the way.
So there are logical reason, there are mission reasons, there are mystical
reasons.
Page 30 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 32 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	33
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Full size version available as Example
#23 in the Exhibits
You may pick one from any of the columns you
like depending on your audience or you may mix
them. You may find a way to put all three of them
together but it is an important copy element.
I'll show you a couple of examples. This is also
instructive for 	a different reason.
This is very current copy. In actual deliverable it
will look like the 	“Let 	Obama 	Make You Rich”
piece, but this is a health piece, so it will be
personalized to the person. It 	drives 	them 	to a
PURLs 	site; 	it's all about pain relief.
Let me give you the important lines that are in the
category of copy we're talking about.
Hi, I'm Doctor John Frank, an MD in private practice, with important news
for you if you suffer from chronic, continuing 	joint pain, back pain, or
arthritis pain.
I'm also a retired professional football player with a Super Bowl ring from
the San Francisco 49ers from playing on the same team as greats like Joe
Montana and Dwight Clark.
As 	an aside, 	that's important because nobody knows 	who 	the hell John Frank is;
but everybody at this age knows who Joe Montana is. So 	by renting John Frank, I
got Joe Montana. I got a picture of Frank with Joe Montana … we can't afford Joe
Montana!
Here's the key line:
As a football player I promise you I know about pain first hand.
I understand you, I appreciate your struggle; that's why I'm here.
Page 31 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 33 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	34
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
"There's not a pro ballplayer alive who doesn't suffer neck, back, knee or
other pain, not just while they play but after they retire. 	As they age most
of us develop arthritis…….
…while opening a jar or tying a shoelace, gardening, golfing, hoisting your
grandkid into a car seat. I get it and I am here with real help.
By the way those items 	– 	opening a jar, tying a shoelace, gardening, golfing,
hoisting your grandkid into a car seat 	– 	are 	the exact items that surveys of
arthritis sufferers complain about, ranked in the order in which they ranked
them.
Copy is not accidental. Not great copy.
You don't just sit down and throw shit up in the air and whatever order it comes
down in, you put it in the copy. Not if you want to win and not if you want to
win in tough categories.
So, I get it, I am here with real help.
Next sentence, if you want 	to steal words, the start of the next sentence is a good
one.
What I'd like you to have…
Those are great words. Not, by the way, what I'd like to sell you 	or 	what I'd like
you to buy. No, “What I'd like you to have.”
Now, just as an aside, an interesting piece of trivia about this; 	this version sells
glop, not information. So, toward the end of the postcard, it shifts into:
I assure you as a doctor this is very different from any off-the-shelf products
at the drugstore, products you see advertised 	on TV or in magazines and it
is safer than prescription drugs because …
So this sells glop. Another version 	of it sells information:
Page 32 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 34 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	35
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
What I'd like you to have is a copy of 	The Big Book of Pain-Free Living.
This giant book is full of expert advice 	about things you can do for yourself
that really work pain relief miracles. Foods to eat, foods never to eat…….
There are ways to get a better night's sleep, wake up rested and ready to go
at any age.
This big book is the most complete, most authoritative, most useful guide
book of pain relief ever compiled.
The rest of it's all 	the same, it’s just 	one sells glop, the other one sells book.
Just as a marketing lesson, this split tests 	and it's split test mixed.
The bonus with the glop is the book. 	The bonus with the book is your first
month of glop free. Then we start whacking your card forevermore until you
finally die of your ailment and keep delivering your 	glop.
So, that's split test, split test, split test, split test, then of course you're splitting
against 	lists 	so this is like a 25 different things split test matrix and about 100
grand 	to 	actually run the test to know something.
You could do it cheaper in a 	different way, but I just wanted to tell you what was
going on.
So, there's a reason for this guy arriving to tell you this story. It has little
elements of, 	“I'm mad as hell.” 	It has a little element of mysticism, new, etc.; but
its main element is, 	“I'm a guy who has the same pain you got, I get it, I finally
found something that works, why keep it to myself? That's why I'm here.”
He 	is 	also 	kind of a trifecta because he's an MD, he's an athlete and he's into
alternative health.
Page 33 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 35 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	36
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Here's 	one that’s 	more logical standard, ordinary; this 	is a control piece for
many years from Newsweek 	(Example #24 in the Exhibits):
Dear 	Reader
If 	the list upon which I found your name is any indication, this is not the
first nor will it be the last subscription letter you receive.
So we have told them we found them on a list of subscribers, that's why you're
getting a subscription pitch. This is 	from Agora. This is an 	Oxford Club control
(Example #25 in the Exhibits):
Dear 	Fellow Investor
That's a good word by the way, add it to your word list. Make yourself the peer.
So 	"Dear Success 	Magazine reader," 	what's better? 	"Dear 	Fellow Success
Magazine reader."
Oh 	we're both Success 	Magazine readers. That's nice.
Dear Fellow Investor,
You 	have been chosen from a select list to receive an invitation into what
must be the world's most remarkable and profitable financial alliance.
“You have 	been chosen from a select list…” the guy gets it, 	“Okay, they went and
found me on a list.” 	Now 	he 	may have some curiosity about what list but we
have appeased his curiosity about, and we have given him a reason why we are
arriving to talk to him now.
Building Trust
So let's up wrap up with the last big hurdle, or the big overarching hurdle, which
is how we get to a sufficient amount of trust for someone to pull the trigger and
hand us money.
This is not new information, it's in books; 	but I want to tie it to this for you.
Page 34 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 36 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	37
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
The first thing to know is that the problem for most people is not just distrust of
you, it is not that they don't trust salespeople, it’s they
don’t trust themselves with salespeople.
During the bleak years of real estate, the biggest problem
that marketers to agents 	– 	not the 	Get Rich in Real Estate
to investor guys but guys like my client Craig 	Proctor 	and
Kinder and Reese, 	who 	market to licensed agents 	– 	their
biggest problem was, all of a sudden nobody would open
their e-mail.
Why? Two reasons:
One, a pre-belief that there's nothing that could be done right now to improve
anything and B, I don't trust myself to read the e-mail because I might buy
something and I don't want to buy anything.
Therefore I won't even tempt myself.
I like trying not to drive near a donut shop. I know I can't trust myself. I don't
stop at a convenience store to get a cup of coffee in our area because they've all
got Krispy Kremes in the counter when you walk in 	to buy 	the coffee.
Guess who ain't just getting a cup of coffee? I'll only eat half, you know. I'll jog it
off later!
So, people don't trust themselves to even read your sales letter. That's where
they are, so it's not just about you, it's about them. That's how extreme it is.
There's nine gates to 	customer commitment 	that all have to do with finally
getting to trust. This list is in the 	No B.S. 	Trust-Based Marketing 	book but you
want to tie it to this.
Here's what people are trying to figure out:
The problem isn’t
just 	distrust of
salespeople… people
don’t trust
themselves with
salespeople.
Page 35 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 37 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	38
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Is he 	“for real”? If 	it's in a person’s 	voice, if you're
writing from you or you're writing for somebody in a
person’s 	voice, does 	he sound authentic to me? Does
he sound real to me?
That's why sometimes a little bit of candor is okay if
you do it, you wrap it properly.
Is 	this a person or 	is this a made-up, fabricated
character?
Some of the best platform salespeople, and
understand that’s 	“copy” 	you see… the best platform
salespeople don't get up 	there and do stuff
spontaneously.
They've scripted this thing.
The best ones work to a great degree because people
see them as being authentic 	– 	not 	perfect by the way.
Is he telling me the truth? Can I believe him? Is there believability to what I'm
hearing 	or 	what I'm reading?
If you go back to my, 	“Enough 	Learning, How About Some Doing?” get 	this line:
Yet I will tell you my own study of this shows fewer than 15% of the people
who try succeed.
Now, 	on the surface, 	you would think, why do we want to remind people that
85% of the people who take a whack at this fail?
Well, because the minute they hear it, they say 	“Well, this guy's telling me the
truth.”
When you present the ugly part… you 	have to be careful how you do it but,
when you do it, 	up goes believability.
NINE GATES TO CUSTOMER
COMMITMENT
1. 	Is this guy 	“for real”?
(Authenticity)
2. 	Is he telling me the truth?
(Believability)
3. 	Is he knowledgeable and
competent? 	(Credibility)
4. 	Is 	he appropriate for me?
(Feasibility of 	Relationship)
5. 	Is he listening or is he just
peddling? 	(Customized
Solutions)
6. 	Overall, can he 	been relied on?
(Safety)
7. 	Do I understand enough about
what he's going to do 	for me?
(Comfort)
8. 	Am I making the best choice v
other choices? (Superiority)
9. 	Am I paying a fair price? (Value)
Page 36 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 38 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	39
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
Is he 	knowledgeable and competent? 	People want to know, 	if they're going to
give you money, do you actually know what the hell you're doing, can you
actually help them, are you competent?
This is important … is he 	appropriate for me? It's called feasibility of
relationship 	– 	which 	is why affinity matches in the first place are so important.
Different people resonate with different people.
As incredible as it may seem to you, there are people who
actually are totally turned off by me! 	This is one of the
great mysteries of life but it exists 	and they may really like
somebody else.
So, what are people trying to figure out? They're trying to figure out, is this a
feasible relationship? Am I going to like being in a working relationship, in a
coaching environment, in a dialog with this person?
Number five 	– 	it's very hard to do at a distance 	– 	is 	he listening or is he just
peddling? People want customized solutions, not 	“one size fits”. It's the
toughest kind of copy to write, but it is there.
Number six, is he reliable? 	Is he still going to be around six months from now?
Because they're looking for safety.
Seven, do I understand enough about what he's going to do to be comfortable? I
don’t really need to know how you move the money through the purple pipe
and the pink pipe but I kind of got to at least know there's pipes.
Am I making the best comparative choice? Obviously you don't want anybody
going comparison shopping, so you need to answer this question for them in
your copy one way or another.
Last, am I paying a fair price? Very few people actually care about, 	“Am I paying
the cheapest price?”
Different people
resonate with
different people.
Page 37 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 39 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	40
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
The only people who really care about that, it's the governing item for about 12-
14%, 	depending on the economy, of the population and it is the people who
have no alternative whatsoever.
They have to get the cheapest price because they have to get the cheapest price
because they are poor and probably none of us want them as customers anyway.
If you want poor people as customers, that's social work. Get some sandals, get
a robe, head out! 	But we don't want poor people as customers.
Now some of them will sneak in and that's okay because sometimes we can't
decide. There's people 	here 	who entered for the first time poor and are no
longer poor.
If 	we have a choice, better to start with somebody who can afford
to buy something that isn't necessarily the cheapest price.
So that's what people want to know.
Wrapping Up
So to wrap this 	up, copy that will persuade people to buy intangibles that have
relatively no intrinsic value… 	it's not a Veg-O-Matic; 	it's not a thing we hang on
the wall and admire. It's not a piece of furniture we sit in.
That's not the business we're in.
The copy that sells that has to get into people's 	psyche and
emotions and has to manipulate the things that already are in
there to an enormous degree.
Now, I have one last thing for you. It loops 	all the way back around to our
ethical considerations.
Here's one of the difficult things about this business.
Page 38 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 40 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	41
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
We know in advance that many, probably the majority will not get the promised
outcomes. If we play fair, we are delivering something to them they could get
the promised outcomes from, but we know in advance most won't.
In one of my earliest businesses in this industry, we actually produced audio
cassette programs, 	we were a manufacturer for all sorts of speakers and
companies.
We used to joke all the time that, 	in reality, if you're manufacturing an eight-
cassette program for somebody, we really only needed to duplicate the first tape
and then put seven blanks in.
If you ship 10,000 of those albums, you would hear from roughly 8, 10, 12
people who would complain that they had blank tapes. You then apologize for
the manufacturing defect and send them out and everybody else, no harm no
foul, because they never listened to them anyway.
Now, we never actually did that 	– 	except 	once or twice by accident 	– 	which 	is
how I know what the actual percentage is of people calling saying, “We got blank
tapes.” 	We were a pretty dysfunctional company!
So, we know in advance that the majority are not going to get the promised
outcomes.
To succeed, to push as hard as you can push, to sell
as effectively as you can sell, this can't bother you.
It is irrelevant, it is not your responsibility or my
responsibility.
You 	didn't have the fun of giving birth to them; 	they
ain't going to come visit you in the old folks home; 	you do not have that level of
parental responsibility for your customers.
We 	know in advance
that the majority are
not going to get the
promised outcomes.
Page 39 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 41 of 42 --

MIND-CONTROL 	COPY 	PAGE 	42
© 2014 GKIC 	Part 	2
You only have the responsibility to play fair, if they followed
directions.
Now, here's the trouble. We can't predict which one will.
Often it's surprising, therefore we can't prejudge them, we can't soft sell some
who appear weak and wounded to us and hard sell people who have money
falling out of their pocket and so it won't 	hurt anybody to take some of it.
No, no, no, no, no; we’ve 	got to get everyone we decide is in our target market,
we’ve 	got to do everything humanly possible to get them and then let them sort
themselves out as they will.
So, the end piece of advice I have for you about copy is don't pull a punch. Do
not pull a punch. 	Use everything you got to make the sale and then let them sort
themselves out.
Page 40 of 40
www.getwsodownload.com

-- 42 of 42 --
